Amended Notice And Motion To Strike Complaint As Against Public Policy
Off Calendar Nov-18-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES- ATTY. LAURA HESS OF THE LAW FIRM OF KRING & CHUNG, LLP, TEL. NO. (949) 261-7700 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE;
ATTY. PHILIP F. SHINN OF FOX ROTHSCHILD LLP, TEL. NO. 364-5540 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS AND MOVING PARTIES.

RULING - A R G U E D; DEFENDANT THORNTON, TAYLOR, BECKER & SHINN'S Amended Motion To Strike Complaint As Against Public Policy IS GRANTED. THE PLAINTIFF HAS NOT PRESENTED ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE THAT WOULD PERMIT OR RAISES A PRIMA FACIE CASE OF MALICE. Prevailing party is to prepare a form of order. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen CSR # 8189 =301/PJB/ER

Amended Notice And Motion To Strike Complaint As Against Public Policy
Off Calendar Oct-27-2008 Continued To Nov-18-08 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 301
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS SURE BUILDING PRODUCTS INC., DELTA BUILDING PRODUCTS LTD., OMEGA ROOF INC.'S Amended Motion To Strike Complaint As Against Public Policy IS CONTINUED TO NOVEMBER 18, 2008 ON THE COURT'S OWN MOTION. Judge: Paul H. Alvarado, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR #8189 (301/PHA)

For Ex-Parte Application To Continue Hearing On Dendant Thornton Taylor Becker & Shinn'S Motion To Strike Complaint As Against Public Policy (Anti-Slapp Motion) For Discovery Purposes, Or Alternatively To Shorten Time For Hearing Motion
Off Calendar Aug-15-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Telephonic Appearance:
Laura Hess, Esq., for Plaintiff and moving party: Sure Building
Philip Shinn, Esq., for defendant and moving party: Thornton Taylor Becker & Shinn

HEARING HELD: Ex Parte Application To Continue Hearing On Defendant Thornton Taylor Becker & Shinn's Motion To Strike Complaint As Against Public Policy (Anti-SLAPP Motion) For Discovery Purposes, or alternatively, To Shorten Time For Hearing The Motion is denied.
The court finds that additional discovery was not sought in a timely fashion. The Anti-SLAPP motion was filed on July 31, 2008. Opposition was filed on August 13, 2008. This application was brought two days after filing the opposition.
The court further finds that there would be prejudice to Defendants as moving party proposes to take depositions, including the deposition of trial counsel in the underlying case, which by necessity would fall during the trial of the underlying case which is set for August 18, 2008. As noted in the order setting aside the consolidation of this case with the underlying case, the problems in getting certain discovery in June was caused by Plaintiffs' decision to name trial counsel as a defendant in this action.
In ruling upon this application to shorten time, the court does not make a finding that additional discovery is not needed to oppose the Anti-SLAPP motion; and Plaintiff is not barred from renewing their request as articulated in their opposition to the Anti-SLAPP motion. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Apr-08-2008 Vacated

Off Calendar Apr-08-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Justin Reden, Esq., appearing by telephone; for defendant and moving party: Nam Chung Cho, Kyung-Chae Park and Chu Hyuk Yoon
Marie Appel, Esq., for plaintiff and opposing party: Sure Building Products, Delta Building Products, Inc., and Omega Roof Inc.


AFTER HEARING, plaintiffs Nam Chung Cho, Kyung-Chae Park, and Chu Hyuk Yoon motion to compel further responses to form interrogatories 15.1 is denied as moot. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN. Not reported.

Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses To Special Interrogatories From Defft Delta Building Products, Ltd
Off Calendar Mar-25-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: NO APPEARANCES. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Plaintiff Chu Hyuk Yoon's Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses To Special Interrogatories From Defendant Delta Building Products, Ltd. is granted as they seek a further response to Interrogatory Number 1. MEDIATION ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Feb-11-2008 Continued To Mar-12-08 At 10:00 A.M. In Dept 212

Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses And Production Of Documents From Deft Sure Building Products, Inc. And Request For Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $2,250.00
Off Calendar Dec-13-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: NO APPEARANCES. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Plaintiff Chu Hyuk Yoon's Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses, And Production Of Documents From Defendant Sure Building Products, Inc. is moot in part and granted in part.
The motion is moot as supplemental responses have been served. The court will not determine the adequacy of those responses on this motion. Counsel must meet and confer as to any responses which are believed to be inadequate.
The motion is granted as to Request For Admission Numbers 10 and 11 and Special Interrogatory Number 28 as the objections are overruled and ordered stricken. The responses otherwise are adequate.
The motion is granted as to Special Interrogatory Number 1. The objections are overruled and ordered stricken. The response is inadequate.
The court finds that the late responses and the failure to respond fully were a misuse of the discovery process. Sanctions have been assessed and set forth in a total amount for the nine motions in Plaintiff Nam Chung Cho's Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses, And Production Of Documents From Defendant Sure Building Products, Inc.; And Request For Monetary Sanctions. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

Notice Of Motion For Protective Order And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Oct-01-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:


Appearance:
Kenneth W. Chung, Esq., for Kenneth W. Chung, Plaintiff/Opposing Party
Phillip F. Shinn, Esq., for Sure Building Products, Inc., Defendant/Opposing Party

Hearing held: Defendant Sure Building Products, Inc.'s Motion For Order To Compel Attendance And Testimony And Production Of Documents At Oral Deposition Of Plaintiff is Denied.
